title: Upstart Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Upstart reported Q4 2024 revenue of approximately $219 million, up 56% year-over-year. The company achieved its second consecutive positive adjusted EBITDA quarter with $38.8 million in adjusted EBITDA at an 18% margin. GAAP diluted earnings per share was negative $0.03, while adjusted diluted earnings per share was $0.26 for the quarter. Full year 2024 net revenue was $637 million, up 24% from 2023, with total fee revenue of $635 million, up 13% year-over-year. Loan originations reached 697,092 loans totaling $5.9 billion, up 28% year-over-year. CEO Dave Girouard highlighted the Payment Transition Model (PTM) as a breakthrough that considers intermediate delinquency states, improving model accuracy and enabling consideration of loans that are delinquent but not charged off. New product categories grew rapidly with auto and HELOC growing about 60% sequentially and the small dollar relief product growing 115% quarter-on-quarter. For 2025, the company projects total revenues of approximately $1 billion with $920 million in fee revenue and adjusted EBITDA margin of 18%. The company expects GAAP net income to be at least breakeven for 2025, with Q1 2025 revenue expected around $200 million. Strategic priorities for 2025 include 10x leadership in AI through accelerated model innovation, prepare funding supply for rapid growth, return to GAAP net income profitability in H2 2025, and achieve best rates and best process for all. The company completed $1.3 billion in commitment upsizes and a $150 million personal loan warehouse facility, reflecting confidence from capital partners in the platform.
